title: streptanthus insignis subsp. insignis
intro: Streptanthus insignis subsp. insignis, also known as the Sierra jewelflower or Sierra streptanthus, is a flowering plant species native to the Sierra Nevada mountain range in California and Nevada. It is a perennial herb in the broccoli family, characterized by its bright yellow and purple flowers, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against cell damage. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote overall health and well-being by reducing stress, improving sleep, and supporting the immune system.
